source_key: 11.1 Density is not the same as no gaps
title: Density is not the same as no gaps
role: Visually separate infinite crowdedness from completeness.
prompt_core: Create a split image. One side shows a line segment crowded with endlessly many rational markers between any two points, yet with a subtle missing target or hole. The other side shows a complete line where a uniquely determined point is allowed to exist as the limit of refinement. The mood should be conceptual clarity, not mysticism. Deep blue, pale light, disciplined composition.
caption: Being dense everywhere is not the same as having no gaps.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "11.1 Density is not the same as no gaps".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Visually separate infinite crowdedness from completeness.

Layout intent:
S — Split-screen corrective plate

Style direction:
density-vs-completeness plate

Core image brief:
Create a split image. One side shows a line segment crowded with endlessly many rational markers between any two points, yet with a subtle missing target or hole. The other side shows a complete line where a uniquely determined point is allowed to exist as the limit of refinement. The mood should be conceptual clarity, not mysticism. Deep blue, pale light, disciplined composition.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
Being dense everywhere is not the same as having no gaps.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

title: Geometry demands √2
role: Show that the diagonal is a real geometric object before it is a decimal expansion.
prompt_core: Illustrate a simple unit square with its diagonal drawn brightly and undeniably present, while the side sits as the chosen unit. The diagonal should feel physically real and geometrically unavoidable, as though it is demanding a place on the number line whether arithmetic is ready or not. Clean stone or parchment texture, quiet authority.
caption: The diagonal exists first; the number system must catch up.

source_key: 11.3 Nested intervals: defining a point by surviving every refinement
title: Nested intervals: defining a point by surviving every refinement
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Make the shrinking-interval idea visually unforgettable.
prompt_core: Show a sequence of shrinking intervals on a number line or a corridor of progressively tighter transparent frames all locking onto one single surviving point. The visual should feel like repeated refinement converging without anxiety, each narrower frame increasing certainty. Slightly surreal but precise. Keep room for optional labels like 1.4, 1.41, 1.414 if overlaid later.
caption: A real number can be defined by what survives every successful refinement.
overlay: Overlay numeric interval labels after generation.

title: Connection back to Eudoxus
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
role: Reveal the family resemblance between proportion tests and nested-interval completeness.
prompt_core: Design a bridge image linking two seemingly different procedures: on one side, Eudoxus-style comparison tests; on the other, nested intervals squeezing toward a point. In the center, show the shared abstract pattern of an object being identified by all the finite tests it passes. This should feel like a deep structural echo across chapters, almost philosophical but still concrete.
caption: Different chapters are reusing the same deeper method.

source_key: 11.5 Zeno’s shadow and why infinite subdivision does not destroy finiteness
title: Zeno’s shadow and why infinite subdivision does not destroy finiteness
role: Defuse the intuition that infinitely many pieces must make a length infinite.
prompt_core: Illustrate a segment repeatedly subdivided into halves, quarters, eighths, and so on, with all pieces still visibly reassembling into the same unchanged whole. The image should reconcile the eye: infinitely many finer descriptions do not make the object longer. Calm, exact, almost meditative, avoiding paradox theater.
caption: More pieces do not mean more total length.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. title: Historical note: rigor took time
role: Historicize the long path from geometric intuition to formal completeness.
prompt_core: Create a visual timeline montage moving from Greek geometric intuition to nineteenth-century rigor, with figures or symbols associated with completeness, limits, and real-number foundations emerging slowly across time. Keep it elegant and spare, not a crowded museum wall. The point is patience: rigor was built, not dropped from the sky.
caption: The modern real line took centuries to become fully explicit.

title: Thought experiments
role: Invite the reader to inhabit shrinking intervals and the unchanged whole.
prompt_core: Show a small set of nested boxes, a line segment split finer and finer, and one unwavering point holding still through all the narrowing. The sketch should feel quiet and anti-anxious, as though infinity is being disciplined into something graspable. Minimal, lucid, almost therapeutic for learners who fear the infinite.
caption: Let the intervals shrink; watch what remains.
